title: Quiz: Random Circuit Sampling
description: Test your understanding of random circuit sampling

questions.question: What is the Porter-Thomas distribution?

options.text: Exponential distribution of output probabilities for random circuits

questions.explanation: For random circuits, output probabilities follow Porter-Thomas: P(p_x) = 2^n * exp(-2^n * p_x).

questions.question: Why is anti-concentration important for RCS hardness?

options.text: It ensures gates are evenly distributed
It prevents probability concentration on few bitstrings

questions.explanation: Anti-concentration prevents classical approximation by spreading probability across outputs.

questions.question: What determines tensor network simulation cost?
questions.explanation: Tensor network cost scales as O(2^tw) where tw is tree-width.

questions.question: For 2D grid of n qubits with depth d, what is approximate tree-width?
questions.explanation: For 2D grid, tree-width ~ O(min(sqrt(n), d) * sqrt(n)).

questions.question: Computing a single output amplitude is what complexity?
questions.explanation: Exact amplitude computation is #P-hard via connection to permanents.

questions.question: What happens to PH if approximate RCS is in BPP?
questions.explanation: Approximate RCS in BPP would collapse PH via Stockmeyer's argument.

questions.question: The fSim gate in Sycamore is equivalent to CNOT.
questions.explanation: fSim is a two-parameter gate combining iSWAP and CZ, more entangling than CNOT.
